channel.title: Vitamin D Far From Equator on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Vitamin D Far From Equator on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Very low levels of vitamin D in the Arctic (only 1% of teens have 30 ng level)
item.description:
• Low vitamin D levels among children and adolescents in an Arctic population

Low vitamin D levels among children and adolescents in an Arctic population

Scandinavian Journal of Public Health Vol 51, Issue 7 https://doi.org/10.1177/14034948221092287

Svetlana Malyavskaya, Galina Kostrova and Аndrey Lebedev+1

Aims:

We aimed to study the vitamin D status of the population of Arkhangelsk, a city in northwestern Russia.

Methods:

A cross-sectional study was conducted to estimate serum 25(OH)D concentrations in Arkhangelsk residents, including 55 neonates and their mothers, 214 children <3 years, 191 schoolchildren (7–8 years), 403 adolescents (13–17 years), 260 university students (18–22 years) and 85 adults (24–60 years). The data were collected from March 2013 to November 2014 and from January 2016 to May 2016.

item.title: 1 in 5 Canadians have inadequate Vitamin D (less than 16 ng), far more if dark skinned – Canada
item.description:
• Vitamin D Status of People 3 to 79 Years of Age from the Canadian Health Measures Survey 2012-2019
      • VitaminDWiki – Is 50 ng of vitamin D too high, just right, or not enough

Vitamin D Status of People 3 to 79 Years of Age from the Canadian Health Measures Survey 2012-2019

J Nutr. 2023 Apr;153(4):1150-1161. doi: 10.1016/j.tjnut.2023.02.026

item.title: Depression in Icelandic men 2.5 X more likely if vitamin D deficient
item.description:
• Depression and serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D in older adults living at northern latitudes – AGES-Reykjavik Study
      • See also Iceland and Vitamin D - many studies

Depression and serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D in older adults living at northern latitudes – AGES-Reykjavik Study

Journal of Nutritional Science (2015), vol. 4, e37, page 1 of 8

Cindy M. Imai. Thorhallur I. Halldorsson, Gudny Eiriksdottir, Mary F. Cotch, Laufey Steingrimsdottir, Inga Thorsdottir, Lenore J. Launer, Tamara Harris, Vilmundur Gudnason and Ingibjorg Gunnarsdottir

item.title: Health can be greatly improved by weekly 50,000 IU of Vitamin D - video and transcript
item.description:
Presented in Iceland March 4, 2023

YouTube (22 minutes in English)     Charts and transcript in English     Smelltu hér til að sjá töflur á íslensku

    • (1) Does Less Sun Mean More Disease? - 5-minute video
    • (2) Reykjavík has the longest Vitamin D winter of the world’s capitals
    • (3) The single best supplement/nutrient to improve your health is Vitamin D
    • (4) 3 forms of Vitamin D in the blood: the final form is a Hormone
    • (5) Some hospitals are noticing: Higher cost if low Vitamin D
    • (6) Fewer Health Problems if more vitamin D Pregnancy
    • (7) Wide range of response to Vitamin D: 50K Vitamin D weekly
    • (8) 26 Health problems fought by bi-weekly 50K Vitamin D
    • (9) 48 Health problems fought by weekly 50K Vitamin D
    • (10) Health Problems fought by 50K Vitamin D: biweekly, weekly
    • (11) Health Problems fought quickly (months) or slowly (years)
    • (12) Only 4 pills per month for normal-weight adult
    • (13) Almost 2 months to respond to 50K weekly unless start with loading dose
    • (14) Obese need 2.5 times as much Vitamin D
    • (15) Vitamin D dose varies with weight and obesity
    • (17) Vitamin D helps you lose weight when you stress your body
    • (18) Why Vitamin D weekly is better than daily
    • (19) Doctors often reluctant to prescribe Vitamin D because:

(1) Does Less Sun Mean More Disease? - 5-minute video

Video in English  video with Icelandic captions

item.title: Iceland and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Iceland discussion of weekly 50,000 IU of vitamin D - March 2023
  • <a href="/posts/health-can-be-greatly-improved-by-weekly-50000-iu-of-vitamin-d-video-and-transcr-y-and-vitamin-d" style=“color: red; text-decoration: underline;” title=“This post/category does not exist yet: Health can be greatly improved by weekly 50,000 IU of Vitamin D - video and transcript - March 2023))
  • {img type=“attId” attId=“20060” width=“400”} Details: Age: 30-85 Gender: Men and Women Vitamin D Levels (nmol/L): 46.1 Number of participants: 944 {FONT(size=“20”)}{ATTACH(inline=“1” id=“20059” icon=“1”)}Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki {ATTACH}{FONT}
  • !!!!!Icelanders supplementing had 19 nmol higher levels in the winter (~1,000 IU) *((Same dose of vitamin D for everyone is virtually impossible - Dec 2015))
      • See also: Depression in Icelandic men 2.5 X more likely if vitamin D deficient – Nov 2015
      • Cancer is a leading cause of Icelandic adult death (Note: vit D prevents many cancers)
      • 100-year history of Vitamin D: from discovery thru COVID - in Icelandic with English Translation
      • Icelanders (like others) with dementia have low levels of vitamin D - April 2020
      • Fewer than 9% of Icelandic ICU patients had more than 30 ng of Vitamin D - June 2016
      • Vitamin-D homeostasis amongst adult Icelandic population - 2004 (46 nmol average)
      • 48 nmol = Vitamin D levels of Children in Iceland - 2020
      • Only 13% of Icelandic children had repeated Vitamin D tests > 50 nmol May 2020
      • Vitamin D status and current policies to achieve adequate vitamin D intake in the Nordic countries
      • Icelandic Mental Health Survey - Jan 2023
      • Vitamin D in northern latitudes: Intake and status in Icelandic children - thesis - 2018
      • Vitamin D levels crash in Icelandic children by age 6 - Feb 2016
      • Top Icelandic disease concerns (Global Burden of Disease) - 2010
      • Vitamin D intake from cod = ~400 IU daily average - not nearly enough for aduls
      • Omega-3 intake from cod = ~300 mg/day
      • VitaminDWiki - Many diseases increase with latitude (low UV, Vitamin D) charts with Iceland
      • See also VitaminDWiki
      • 32 publications with (vitamin D keyword or Reykjavik) by Icelandic authors as of Oct 2023
      • 10,200 Iceland and Vitamin D articles in Google Scholar as of Sept 2023
      • Chronic pain in Iceland
      • Does less sun mean more disease? 215 days of winter in Iceland (No vitamin D from the sun)
    • COVID and Iceland
      • Vaccine could have tripled Iceland’s Covid death rate - Nov 2023
      • 4% Excess deaths in Iceland for ages 0-64 (due to COVID vaccine) - Aug 2023

Iceland discussion of weekly 50,000 IU of vitamin D - March 2023

<a href="/posts/health-can-be-greatly-improved-by-weekly-50000-iu-of-vitamin-d-video-and-transcr-y-and-vitamin-d" style=“color: red; text-decoration: underline;” title=“This post/category does not exist yet: Health can be greatly improved by weekly 50,000 IU of Vitamin D - video and transcript - March 2023))

!!!!Iceland Vitamin D level 46 nmol (18 ng) - 2005 Relationship between serum parathyroid hormone levels, vitamin D sufficiency, and calcium intake. JAMA 294:2336-2341 URL: http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16278362 Steingrimsdottir L et al

item.title: Russia and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
Low D due to high latitude (llttle UVB get thru so much atmosphere)

Low D due to alcohol

Low D due to smoking

Low D due to low Vitamin D in poor food (and very few seafoods)

      • The first Russian multicenter non-interventional registry study to study the incidence of vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency in Russian Federation - 2021
      • Russia - Aug 2021 84% are deficient ( <20 ng)
      • Vitamin D status of the Russian adult population from 2013 to 2018 - Oct 2022 (24 ng?)
      • Chart of Vitamin D levels in Europe - including Russia (in Orange)
      • VitaminDWiki - Many countries: >70% have < 20 ng

The first Russian multicenter non-interventional registry study to study the incidence of vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency in Russian Federation - 2021

Ter Arkh . 2021 Oct 15;93(10):1209-1216. doi: 10.26442/00403660.2021.10.201071

item.title: Free 1,600 IU Vitamin D daily during pregnancy helps a lot (natives in Alaska)
item.description:
• Impact of a Prenatal Vitamin D Supplementation Program on Vitamin D Deficiency, Rickets and Early Childhood Caries in an Alaska Native Population

Impact of a Prenatal Vitamin D Supplementation Program on Vitamin D Deficiency, Rickets and Early Childhood Caries in an Alaska Native Population

Nutrients 2022, 14(19), 3935; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu14193935

Vitamin D levels 20-40 weeks later

Background: Early childhood rickets increased in Alaska Native children after decreases in vitamin D-rich subsistence diet in childbearing-aged women. We evaluated the impact of routine prenatal vitamin D supplementation initiated in Alaska’s Yukon Kuskokwim Delta in Fall 2016.

item.title: Arctic school children in Russia: only 1 in 11 has more than 30 ng of Vitamin D
item.description:
• Low vitamin D levels among children and adolescents in an Arctic population
      • VitaminDWiki - After years of adding vitamin D, Finland now has 38 ng levels - March 2022

Low vitamin D levels among children and adolescents in an Arctic population

https://doi.org/10.1177/14034948221092287 behind $41 paywall

Svetlana Malyavskaya, Galina Kostrova , Alexander V. Kudryavtsev, …

Aims:

We aimed to study the vitamin D status of the population of Arkhangelsk, a city in northwestern Russia.

item.title: Canadian immigrants have lower vitamin D levels and more chronic diseases
item.description:
• Vitamin D and Chronic Diseases among First-Generation Immigrants: A Large-Scale Study Using Canadian Health Measures Survey (CHMS) Data
      • Risk factors for low immigrant vitamin D

Vitamin D and Chronic Diseases among First-Generation Immigrants: A Large-Scale Study Using Canadian Health Measures Survey (CHMS) Data

Nutrients 2022, 14(9), 1760; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu14091760

by Said Yousef 1,2,*,Ian Colman 1,Manny Papadimitropoulos 3,4,Douglas Manuel 5,6,7,Alomgir Hossain 1,6ORCID,MoezAlIslam Faris 8ORCID andGeorge A. Wells 1,2

Background: Nearly 22% of the Canadian population are first-generation immigrants. We investigated immigrants’ health status and health deterioration over time in terms of the prevalence of chronic diseases (CDs) and their relationship to vitD status.

item.title: Happiest country on earth has a high Vitamin D level (Finland, with little sun)
item.description:
• Is Finland really the happiest country in the world? Finns weigh in.

Is Finland really the happiest country in the world? Finns weigh in.

Washington Post March 31. 2022

  • “For the fifth year in a row, Finland has been named the happiest country in the world by the United Nations-sponsored World Happiness Report.”

Note: Most of the top 10 happiest countries are in Europe and supplement or fortify with vitamin D

item.title: After years of adding vitamin D, Finland now has 38 ng levels
item.description:
• Long-term nutritional trends in the Finnish population estimated from a large laboratory database from 1987 to 2020

Long-term nutritional trends in the Finnish population estimated from a large laboratory database from 1987 to 2020

Scientific Reports volume 12, Article number: 5008 (2022) https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-022-09131-x

Tamara Tuuminen, Mikko Sorsa, Martin Tornudd, Tuija Poussa, Pyry Suonsivu, Eeva Marja Pitkänen, Erkki Antila & Kaarlo Jaakkola

The assessments of malnutrition in adults with MUST or NRS-2002 criteria do not give a detailed insight into the sufficiency of micronutrients. Sufficiency assessment of essential micronutrients on the individual level can be achieved only with laboratory measurements. The aim of this study was to estimate long-term trends in micronutrient sufficiency in the Finnish population with regards to gender and sex covariates. We retrieved from the clinical laboratory database (n = 67,236) all results on whole blood Magnesium, (B-Mg), Manganese (B-Mn), Zinc (B-Zn), Selenium (B-Se) and Copper from erythrocytes (E-Cu) and fasting serum ß-carotenes (fS-BKarot), vitamin A (fS-A-vit), coenzyme Q10 (Ubiquinone, fS-Q10) and serum vitamin D (S-D-25) from the database of clinical laboratory Mineraalilaboratorio Mila Oy from the years 1987–2020. A weak positive linear trend is seen for B-Mg, B-Zn and ln(fS-Q10) both for children and adults, but a moderate linear positive trend was observed for ln(S-D-25) based on correlation between calendar year and ln(S-D-25), R = 0.44 and 0.41, p < 0.001 for adults and children, respectively. Laboratory database is helpful to monitor the nutritional public policy to prevent hidden malnutrition in the society.

item.title: Low Vitamin D if little UVB, supplementation helps (Antarctica) – meta-analysis
Multiple Sclerosis varies with latitude while young
2400 IU of vitamin D needed to get most Northern adults above 20 ng
Middle Eastern women living in Sweden 23X higher risk to be anxious if low vitamin D
95 percent of pregnant women in Finland took vitamin D – PHD Dissertation
item.description:
• Vitamin D in pregnancy and infancy : dietary sources and associations with pregnancy outcomes and infant growth

Vitamin D in pregnancy and infancy : dietary sources and associations with pregnancy outcomes and infant growth

Helena Hauta-alus

HUS Lasten ja nuorten sairaudetLastenklinikkaDoctoral Programme in Clinical ResearchHelsingin yliopisto

Tutkimustuotos: Opinnäyte › Väitöskirja

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Kuvaus

Vitamin D is vital for normal growth and development. Vitamin D is produced endogenously in the skin after sunlight exposure or obtained from dietary sources. In Finland, solar radiation is inadequate for cutaneous vitamin D synthesis in winter, leading to a high risk for vitamin D insufficiency, defined by circulating 25-hydroxyvitamin D concentration [25(OH)D] below 50 nmol/l. Poor maternal 25(OH)D has been associated with adverse pregnancy and neonatal outcomes, such as pre-eclampsia, gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM), and low birth weight. Only a few studies have explored the relationship between vitamin D and infant postnatal growth, and these studies show inconsistent results. Further, data on current maternal vitamin D status and infant vitamin D intake in Finland are lacking. The objectives of this thesis were to

item.title: Low Vitamin D in Nordic countries – supplementation, fortification, only 20 ng
item.description:
Vitamin D Status and Current Policies to Achieve Adequate Vitamin D Intake in the Nordic Countries

Scand J Public Health, 1403494819896878 2020 Jan 9, DOI: 10.1177/1403494819896878

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Supplementation- typically only 400 IU, no extra for dark skins

Fortification

Most people recently from Middle East (dark skins) had <20 ng

Suvi T Itkonen 1, Rikke Andersen 2, Anne K Björk 3, Åsa Brugård Konde 4, Hanna Eneroth 4, Maijaliisa Erkkola 1, Kristin Holvik 5, Ahmed A Madar 6, Haakon E Meyer 5 6, Inge Tetens 7, Jóhanna E Torfadóttir 8 9, Birna Thórisdóttir 9 10, Christel J E Lamberg-Allardt 1

item.title: Vitamin D tests for Canadian BC adults can only be ordered by specialists
Multiple Sclerosis incidence is still associated with latitude
item.description:
Latitude continues to be significantly associated with the prevalence of multiple sclerosis: an updated meta-analysis.

J Neurol Neurosurg Psychiatry. 2019 Nov;90(11):1193-1200. doi: 10.1136/jnnp-2018-320189

Simpson S Jr1,2, Wang W3,4, Otahal P2, Blizzard L2, van der Mei IAF2, Taylor BV2.

  • Vitamin D Receptor is associated in over 58 autoimmune studies

  • Overview MS and vitamin D

item.title: Heart Attacks in men 13 percent more likely if 10 degrees further North (Sweden)
Canadian pregnancies have enough vitamin D – if white, summer, supplement, eat meat, etc.
item.description:
Summer Season and Recommended Vitamin D Intake Support Adequate Vitamin D Status throughout Pregnancy in Healthy Canadian Women and Their Newborns.

J Nutr. 2019 Nov 16. pii: nxz276. doi: 10.1093/jn/nxz276.

Perreault M1, Atkinson SA1, Meyre D2,3, Fusch G1, Mottola MF4; BHIP Study Team.

  • 88% white,

  • 86% took supplements,

  • summer pregnancies

  • The study appears unaware that people get 6X more vitamin D from meat and eggs

item.title: Genes evolve to adapt to excess or low vitamin D (Eskimo, Reindeer, etc.)
item.description:
Note: It probably takes many 1,000’s of years for the genes to adapt

  • Eskimos evolved to get and limit Vitamin D from food

  • Inuit and vitamin D

Reindeer genes are 20X better at activating Vitamin D than other mammals

Biological adaptations in the Arctic cervid, the reindeer (Rangifer tarandus) - June 2019

item.title: Many diseases increase with latitude (low UV, Vitamin D)
item.description: Lower Type 1 Diabetes and Cancer Rates Associated with Higher UVB Grassroots Health May 2019

item.title: Half as many teeth problems if higher cord blood level of vitamin D
Increased incidence of 7 cancers if live far from equator (if not take vitamin D)
item.description:
The Role of Vitamin D and Sunlight Incidence in Cancer.

Anticancer Agents Med Chem. 2019 Mar 12. doi: 10.2174/1389557519666190312123212

Câmara AB1, Brandãob IA2.

  • 1 Department of Biophysics and Pharmacology, Bioscience Center, Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte, 59064-741, Natal/RN. Brazil.

  • 2 Metrópole Digital Institute, Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte, 59078-970, Natal/RN. Brazil.

#### Cancers

  • Getting little UV is associated with 15 types of Cancer – Jan 2012

item.title: Vitamin D varies with latitude
item.description:
Vitamin D vs latitude - Chile

Strangely, the above map was in the supplement PDF, not the main PDF

Supplement from: Passive Commuting and Higher Sedentary Time Is Associated with Vitamin D Deficiency in Adult and Older Women: Results from Chilean National Health Survey 2016–2017

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

item.title: Swedish TV Warns 'Dark-Skinned' People, Muslims of Vitamin D Deficiency
item.description:
News Item

“A 2015 study in Borlänge, Sahra’s home town, found that 9 out of 10 pregnant Somali women in Sweden suffer from severe vitamin D deficiency, which, coupled with other risk factors, can contribute to a child developing autism. Incidentally, this diagnosis is three to four times more common among children with Somali background, "

“The number of Muslims living in Sweden has been estimated at around 800,000, or roughly 8 percent of Sweden’s population of about 10 million”

item.title: Multiple Sclerosis incidence varies with latitude (noted in Brazil too)
item.description:
Prevalence of multiple sclerosis in Brazil: A systematic review

Multiple Sclerosis and Related Disorders, Vol 4, # 6, November 2015, Pages 572-579, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.msard.2015.08.004

panelAna Beatriz Calmon Nogueirada Gama PereiraabMaria ClineteSampaio LacativaacFernanda Ferreira Chavesda Costa PereiraadRegina MariaPapais Alvarengaae

Highlights

  • This meta-analysis based on 19 published crude prevalence studies from Brazil has shown that the prevalence of multiple sclerosis is low compared with western countries on the northern hemisphere.

  • In Brazil there is a strong and statistical significant latitudinal gradient with higher prevalence at high southern latitudes. This gradient could not be explained alone by racial differences between the regions.

item.title: Alaska recommends more vitamin D: Infancy 800 IU, Pregnancy 1400 IU (avoid rickets)
item.description:
State workgroup recommends more vitamin D for Alaskan children, pregnant women

“The Alaska Vitamin D Workgroup recently formulated Alaska-specific vitamin D supplement recommendations. It recommends clinicians in the 49th state consider prescribing double the nationally recommended amount for breastfed infants.”

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

See also VitaminDWiki

  • Alaskan Resolutions to increase vitamin D
